title: emaxnls — Project Memory
text: emaxnls implements Emax dose-response regression models pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic analysis. supports: Continuous responses via nonlinear least squares (emax_nls()) Binary responses via logistic Emax regression (emax_logistic()) Stepwise covariate modelling (SCM) via emax_scm_forward() / emax_scm_backward() Simulation fitted models (simulate()) Emax model parameters E0 (baseline), Emax (maximum effect), EC50 (half-maximal concentration), optionally Hill (sigmoidal shape). Parameters EC50 Hill estimated log scale internally (logEC50, logHill), back-transformation available via back_transform = TRUE. package CRAN version 0.1.1; development version 0.1.1.9000. Package website: https://emaxnls.djnavarro.net/

title: Fitting Emax models for continuous outcomes
text: Emax model widely used exposure-response model pharmacometrics. describes smooth, saturating relationship measure drug exposure (dose, concentration, AUC) response. exposure increases response moves away baseline value asymptotes towards maximal effect. appeal model parameters direct pharmacological meaning.

title: Fitting Emax models for continuous outcomes
text: basic (hyperbolic) form, expected response yy exposure xx y=E0+Emaxxx+EC50. y = E_0 + E_{\max}\,\frac{x}{x + EC_{50}}. three structural parameters : E0E_0 — baseline response, .e. expected response zero exposure (x=0x = 0). placebo pre-treatment level. EmaxE_{\max} — maximal effect, largest change baseline drug can produce. x→∞x \\infty response approaches E0+EmaxE_0 + E_{\max}. EC50EC_{50} — exposure produces half maximal effect. x=EC50x = EC_{50} term x/(x+EC50)=1/2x / (x + EC_{50}) = 1/2, response exactly halfway E0E_0 E0+EmaxE_0 + E_{\max}. measure potency: smaller EC50EC_{50} means drug reaches effect lower exposures. EC50EC_{50} strictly positive quantity often spans several orders magnitude, package estimates log scale. Internally model works log⁡EC50\log EC_{50}, keeps parameter unconstrained tends make optimisation better behaved. parameter named logEC50 throughout interface.

title: Fitting Emax models for continuous outcomes
text: hyperbolic model can generalised adding Hill coefficient hh, controls steepness curve: y=E0+Emaxxhxh+EC50h. y = E_0 + E_{\max}\,\frac{x^{h}}{x^{h} + EC_{50}^{\,h}}. h=1h = 1 reduces hyperbolic model. Larger values hh produce steeper, switch-like response. Like EC50EC_{50}, Hill coefficient positive estimated log scale logHill. sigmoidal model requested including logHill term covariate model (see ); without , emax_nls() fits hyperbolic model.

title: Fitting Emax models for continuous outcomes
text: Given structural covariate models, fitting reduces finding parameter values make predicted responses close possible observed responses. Writing f(xi,zi;θ)f(x_i, z_i; \theta) Emax prediction ii-th observation θ\theta full vector coefficients, emax_nls() solves nonlinear least squares problem θ̂=arg⁡minθ∑=1n(yi−f(xi,zi;θ))2. \hat{\theta} = \arg\min_{\theta} \sum_{=1}^{n} \big(y_i - f(x_i, z_i; \theta)\big)^2 . usual assumption independent, normally distributed errors constant variance, least squares solution also maximum likelihood estimate. (supply weights vector via emax_nls_options(), package minimises weighted sum squares instead.) Two features make harder ordinary linear regression: nonlinear. prediction ff linear function parameters (exposure enters x/(x+EC50)x / (x + EC_{50})), closed-form solution. estimate found iterative optimisation. needs starting values. Iterative optimisers must given initial guess every parameter, nonlinear models poor guess can lead non-convergence local optimum.

title: Fitting Emax models for continuous outcomes
text: optimisation algorithm fitting controls set emax_nls_options(), whose result passed opts argument emax_nls(). Three algorithms supported: "gauss" (default) — Gauss-Newton algorithm, equivalent default method stats::nls(). "port" — bounded optimisation using nl2sol routine PORT library. respects lower/upper bounds useful parameters must stay within plausible range. "levenberg" — Levenberg-Marquardt algorithm via minpack.lm::nlsLM(), often robust starting values poor.

Instead Emax model placed log-odds (logit) scale: logit⁡(p)=log⁡(p1−p)=E0+Emaxxx+EC50, \operatorname{logit}(p) = \log\!\left(\frac{p}{1 - p}\right)   = E_0 + E_{\max}\,\frac{x}{x + EC_{50}}, sigmoidal (Hill) version logit⁡(p)=E0+Emaxxhxh+EC50h \operatorname{logit}(p) = E_0 + E_{\max}\,\frac{x^{h}}{x^{h} + EC_{50}^{\,h}} available adding logHill term covariate model, exactly continuous case. structural parameters roles , now live logit scale: E0E_0 baseline log-odds zero exposure, EmaxE_{\max} maximal change log-odds drug can produce. , EC50EC_{50} Hill coefficient estimated log scale logEC50 logHill, structural parameter can carry covariates. logit link connects unbounded linear---logit-scale predictor back probability. Y_i \sim \operatorname{Bernoulli}(p_i), \qquad \Pr(Y_i = y_i) = p_i^{\,y_i}\,(1 - p_i)^{1 - y_i}. Two consequences error model matter estimation. First, likelihood want maximise Bernoulli (log-)likelihood ℓ(θ)=∑=1n[yilog⁡pi+(1−yi)log⁡(1−pi)], \ell(\theta) = \sum_{=1}^{n}\Big[\, y_i \log p_i + (1 - y_i)\log(1 - p_i)\,\Big], logLik() returns deviance() reports −2ℓ(θ)-2\ell(\theta) (binomial deviance). Second, variance Bernoulli observation Var⁡(Yi)=pi(1−pi)\operatorname{Var}(Y_i) = p_i(1 - p_i): constant, depends mean. what: Why not a bare call to nls()?
title: Fitting Emax models for binary outcomes
text: tempting just fit structural Emax curve 0/1 responses nls(), minimising ∑(yi−pi)2\sum_i (y_i - p_i)^2. bad idea several related reasons: wrong error model. Least squares maximum-likelihood estimator Gaussian errors constant variance. Binary data variance pi(1−pi)p_i(1 - p_i), changes mean. Treating every observation equally precise gives statistically inefficient estimates , seriously, standard errors, confidence intervals, pp-values simply wrong. Unbounded predictions. Fitting Emax form directly probability scale places constraint pip_i, nothing stops fitted curve drifting 00 11. logit link removes problem construction. maximum-likelihood estimate. Minimising squared error maximise Bernoulli likelihood, resulting estimates lack consistency efficiency properties rely inference. standard remedy generalised linear models iterative reweighted least squares (IRLS), also known Fisher scoring. idea replace single least-squares problem sequence weighted least-squares problems progressively account mean-variance relationship. iteration, given current linear predictor η\eta fitted probabilities μ=logit⁡−1(η)\mu = \operatorname{logit}^{-1}(\eta), form wi=μi(1−μi)(working weights),zi=ηi+yi−μiwi(working response), w_i = \mu_i(1 - \mu_i) \qquad\text{(working weights)}, \qquad z_i = \eta_i + \frac{y_i - \mu_i}{w_i} \qquad\text{(working response)}, solve weighted least-squares fit working response zz structural model, using weights ww. weights exactly Bernoulli variance function, informative observations -weighted; working response local linearisation link. what: Forward addition piped to backward elimination
title: Stepwise covariate modelling
text: typical workflow forward-backward run: forward addition build model , immediately followed backward elimination prune . functions take fitted model first argument return fitted model, compose naturally native pipe: things worth unpacking . terms chosen. Selection based pp-value criterion. forward step, every candidate term already model added turn compared current model; term smallest pp-value retained, provided pp-value threshold. step repeats remaining candidate clears bar. Backward elimination works reverse: term currently model dropped turn, term removed pp-value exceeds threshold. continuous models pp-value comes FF-test comparing nested models (via anova() underlying nls fits). thresholds differ. standard practice make forward addition permissive backward elimination — 0.010.01 add 0.0010.001 retain. term added looser forward criterion must survive stricter backward criterion, guards terms looked useful presence others. Using stricter backward threshold forward also prevents procedure cycling (adding removing term forever). Reproducibility. Within step candidate terms tested random order, results can depend state random number generator. Passing seed makes run reproducible, . (seed argument currently experimental may change future release.)
code: final_model <- base_model |>   emax_scm_forward(candidates = candidates, threshold = 0.01, seed = 1) |>   emax_scm_backward(candidates = candidates, threshold = 0.001, seed = 1) #> Warning: `nls()` did not converge

title: Stepwise covariate modelling
text: Nothing SCM interface changes binary outcomes. start emax_logistic() base model instead emax_nls() one, emax_scm_forward() / emax_scm_backward() pipeline applies. difference internal: logistic models selection pp-value comes likelihood-ratio chi-squared test rather FF-test, matching Bernoulli likelihood used fit model. binary response rsp_2 generated genuine effects cnt_a bin_d baseline, search recovers . audit log extracted exactly way: two forward iterations accepted term (E0 ~ cnt_a, E0 ~ bin_d), backward step retained .
code: base_logistic <- emax_logistic(   structural_model = rsp_2 ~ exp_1,   covariate_model = list(E0 ~ 1, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1),   data = emax_df )  final_logistic <- base_logistic |>   emax_scm_forward(candidates = candidates, threshold = 0.01, seed = 1) |>   emax_scm_backward(candidates = candidates, threshold = 0.001, seed = 1)  final_logistic #> Structural model: #>  #>   Exposure:       exp_1  #>   Response:       rsp_2  #>   Emax type:      hyperbolic  #>   Response type:  binary (logit link) #>  #> Covariate model: #>  #>   E0:       E0 ~ 1 + cnt_a + bin_d  #>   Emax:     Emax ~ 1  #>   logEC50:  logEC50 ~ 1  #>  #> Model fit: #>  #>   Observations:  400  #>   Residual df:   395  #>   Deviance:      316  #>   AIC:           326  #>  #> Coefficients (95% CI): #>  #>   label             estimate std_error  lower  upper #> 1 E0_cnt_a             0.693    0.0817  0.532  0.853 #> 2 E0_bin_d             1.11     0.289   0.543  1.68  #> 3 E0_Intercept        -5.69     0.624  -6.91  -4.46  #> 4 Emax_Intercept       7.99     2.15    5.09  16.2   #> 5 logEC50_Intercept    9.75     0.504   8.89  10.9   #>  #> Use summary() for hypothesis tests. logistic_history <- emax_scm_history(final_logistic)  accepted_b <- logistic_history$step %in% c("base model", "final model") |   (!is.na(logistic_history$model_updated) & logistic_history$model_updated)  logistic_history[accepted_b, c("iteration", "step", "action", "term_tested",                                "model_tested", "term_p_value", "model_aic")] #> # A tibble: 4 × 7 #>   iteration step        action term_tested model_tested   term_p_value model_aic #>       <int> <chr>       <chr>  <chr>       <chr>                 <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1         0 base model  NA     NA          E0 ~ 1, Emax …    NA             444. #> 2         1 forward     add    E0 ~ cnt_a  E0 ~ 1 + cnt_…     4.53e-25      339. #> 3         2 forward     add    E0 ~ bin_d  E0 ~ 1 + cnt_…     8.47e- 5      326. #> 4         5 final model NA     NA          E0 ~ 1 + cnt_…    NA             326.

title: Stepwise covariate modelling
text: Selection criterion. current implementation selects pp-values . history records model_aic model_bic every candidate, can audit search information criteria even though used drive ; selection AIC criteria may supported future. Threshold choice. forward backward thresholds main levers control. Stricter thresholds yield sparser models. Keeping backward threshold forward threshold recommended avoid cycling. Greediness. Stepwise search greedy guaranteed find globally best subset covariates. audit log valuable precisely makes path search took transparent reproducible. fitting interpreting individual models — continuous binary — see companion model-fitting articles.

what: Nonlinear Least Squares Estimation for Emax Regression Models
title: Nonlinear Least Squares Estimation for Emax Regression Models
text: emaxnls package provides tools nonlinear least squares estimation Emax regression models. supplies clean interface specifying Emax regression models covariates, using nls() underlying tool fitting model. supports least squares estimation using Gauss-Newton algorithm, Levenberg-Marquardt algorithm (via minpack.lm::nls.lm()) ‘nl2sol’ algorithm Port library. Continuous binary response variables supported, iterative reweighted least squares method used produce estimates binary case.

text: binary outcomes, package provides emax_logistic() function, estimates parameters logistic Emax model. model logit-link function binomial family assumed, parameters estimated using iterative reweighted nonlinear least squares procedure.

title: Nonlinear Least Squares Estimation for Emax Regression Models
text: package supports stepwise covariate modeling via forward addition backward elimination. emax_scm_forward() function supports forward addition, emax_scm_backward() function supports backward elimination, syntax designed allow forward-backward procedures piping base model emax_scm_forward() emax_scm_backward().
code: # base model with no covariates base_model <- emax_nls(   structural_model = rsp_1 ~ exp_1,    covariate_model = list(E0 ~ 1, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1),    data = emax_df )  # list of possible consider for E0 and Emax covariate_list <- list(   E0 = c("cnt_a", "cnt_b", "cnt_c", "bin_d", "bin_e"),   Emax = c("cnt_a", "cnt_b", "cnt_c", "bin_d", "bin_e") )  # stepwise covariate modeling with a forward step and a backward step final_mod <- base_model |>    emax_scm_forward(candidates = covariate_list, threshold = .01) |>    emax_scm_backward(candidates = covariate_list, threshold = .001)   # extract the complete history of all models tested during the  # stepwise covariate modeling procedure emax_scm_history(final_mod) #> # A tibble: 22 × 11 #>    iteration attempt step       action term_tested  model_tested model_converged #>        <int>   <int> <chr>      <chr>  <chr>        <chr>        <lgl>           #>  1         0       0 base model <NA>   <NA>         E0 ~ 1, Ema… TRUE            #>  2         1       1 forward    add    E0 ~ cnt_c   E0 ~ 1 + cn… TRUE            #>  3         1       2 forward    add    Emax ~ bin_e E0 ~ 1, Ema… TRUE            #>  4         1       3 forward    add    E0 ~ cnt_b   E0 ~ 1 + cn… TRUE            #>  5         1       4 forward    add    Emax ~ cnt_c E0 ~ 1, Ema… TRUE            #>  6         1       5 forward    add    Emax ~ cnt_a E0 ~ 1, Ema… TRUE            #>  7         1       6 forward    add    Emax ~ bin_d E0 ~ 1, Ema… TRUE            #>  8         1       7 forward    add    E0 ~ cnt_a   E0 ~ 1 + cn… TRUE            #>  9         1       8 forward    add    Emax ~ cnt_b E0 ~ 1, Ema… TRUE            #> 10         1       9 forward    add    E0 ~ bin_e   E0 ~ 1 + bi… TRUE            #> # ℹ 12 more rows #> # ℹ 4 more variables: term_p_value <dbl>, model_aic <dbl>, model_bic <dbl>, #> #   model_updated <lgl>  # show the final model final_mod #> Structural model: #>  #>   Exposure:       exp_1  #>   Response:       rsp_1  #>   Emax type:      hyperbolic  #>   Response type:  continuous #>  #> Covariate model: #>  #>   E0:       E0 ~ 1 + cnt_a  #>   Emax:     Emax ~ 1  #>   logEC50:  logEC50 ~ 1  #>  #> Model fit: #>  #>   Observations:         400  #>   Residual df:          396  #>   Residual std. error:  0.5108  #>   AIC:                  603.6431  #>  #> Coefficients (95% CI): #>  #>   label             estimate std_error lower  upper #> 1 E0_cnt_a             0.486    0.0116 0.463  0.509 #> 2 E0_Intercept         5.05     0.0759 4.91   5.20  #> 3 Emax_Intercept       9.97     0.112  9.75  10.2   #> 4 logEC50_Intercept    8.27     0.0394 8.19   8.35  #>  #> Use summary() for hypothesis tests.

text: default, simultaneous = FALSE, calls stats::confint.nls() emaxnls objects. emaxlogistic objects, profiling approach applied final NLS fit IRLS algorithm convergence. profile likelihood computation fails (can occur sigmoidal models), warning issued Wald intervals returned instead. simultaneous = TRUE, single critical value derived joint multivariate normal distribution standardized parameter estimates (via mvtnorm::qmvnorm()). resulting intervals simultaneous coverage level across parameters wider individual (pointwise) intervals. matches intervals produced summary(object, simultaneous = TRUE). Setting back_transform = TRUE exponentiates confidence limits logEC50 logHill, expressing concentration scale rather log-concentration scale estimated, drops log prefix row names.

text: extracted function accepts data param arguments. default values used fitting model. supplying custom values, data must contain variables used model, param must named numeric vector whose names exactly match returned coef(mod). Scale predictions. emaxnls objects returned function produces predictions response scale (scale outcome variable). emaxlogistic objects structural Emax model parameterized logit scale — logit(p) = E0 + Emax * x / (x + EC50) — emax_fun() applies inverse-logit transformation returning, predictions probability scale. consistent default behavior fitted() predict() emaxlogistic objects. need linear predictor (logit scale) directly, use fitted(object, type = "link") predict(object, type = "link").

title: Construct Emax prediction function from model object — emax_fun
code: mod_c <- emax_nls(   structural_model = rsp_1 ~ exp_1,    covariate_model = list(E0 ~ cnt_a, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1),    data = emax_df,   opts = emax_nls_options(max_time = 10) )  if (emax_converged(mod_c)) {    par <- coef(mod_c)   mod_fn <- emax_fun(mod_c)      # apply the function to a few rows of the original data   mod_fn(data = emax_df[120:125, ], param = par)      # adjust the parameters and re-evaluate   new_par <- par   new_par["E0_Intercept"] <- 0   mod_fn(data = emax_df[120:125, ], param = new_par)  } #> [1]  9.595929  3.412750  8.208382 10.674149  1.200782  8.567196  # for emaxlogistic, the returned function gives probabilities mod_b <- emax_logistic(   structural_model = rsp_2 ~ exp_1,   covariate_model = list(E0 ~ cnt_a, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1),   data = emax_df,   opts = emax_logistic_options(max_time = 10) )  if (emax_converged(mod_b)) {   mod_fn_b <- emax_fun(mod_b)   mod_fn_b(data = emax_df[120:125, ]) } #> [1] 0.73738728 0.40712302 0.38375750 0.92637415 0.03314224 0.39944030

Allowed values differ depending algorithm used quiet quiet=TRUE, messages suppressed weights Numeric vector providing weights observations. specified, weighted least squares used na.action missing values data handled? max_time Maximum elapsed time seconds allowed model fit. optimizer converged within time, terminated model treated non-converged (outcome convergence failure). title: Settings used to estimate Emax model — emax_nls_options
text: present three supported values optim_method: "gauss": Estimate parameters using Gauss-Newton algorithm. equivalent using "default" option nls() "port": Estimate parameters using bounded optimization "nl2sol" algorithm Port library. Equivalent "port" nls() "levenberg": Estimate parameters using Levenberg-Marquardt algorithm. equivalent using nlsLM() "minpack.lm" package. Note Golub-Pereyra algorithm partially linear least-squares (.e. "plinear" option nls()) currently supported Emax regression. Informal testing suggests perform well models, rarely converges. optim_control argument mirrors corresponding control arguments respective optimization methods: "gauss" "port": list match output stats::nls.control() "levenberg": list match output minpack.lm::nls.lm.control() optim_control = NULL, default settings used relevant function.

text: Performs stepwise covariate modeling forward addition (emax_scm_forward()), backward elimination (emax_scm_backward()), sequence. Use emax_scm_history() retrieve history models tested procedure.

title: Stepwise covariate modeling for Emax regression — emax_scm
code: emax_scm_forward(mod, candidates, threshold = 0.01, seed = NULL)  emax_scm_backward(mod, candidates, threshold = 0.001, seed = NULL)  emax_scm_history(mod)

title: Stepwise covariate modeling for Emax regression — emax_scm
text: mod emaxnls object candidates list candidate covariates threshold Threshold addition removal seed Seed RNG state

title: Stepwise covariate modeling for Emax regression — emax_scm
text: object class emaxnls

title: Stepwise covariate modeling for Emax regression — emax_scm
text: candidates argument must named list whose names correspond structural parameters (e.g. E0, Emax) whose values character vectors covariate names consider. See examples illustration. present, covariate selection uses p-values criterion: term added p-value falls threshold (forward) removed p-value exceeds threshold (backward). Selection AIC criteria may supported future. seed argument controls RNG state stochastic components procedure. currently experimental may removed future releases. Every model tested procedure stored internally returned object. Use emax_scm_history() extract record.

title: Stepwise covariate modeling for Emax regression — emax_scm
code: base_model <- emax_nls(   structural_model = rsp_1 ~ exp_1,    covariate_model = list(E0 ~ 1, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1),    data = emax_df,   opts = emax_nls_options(max_time = 10) )  covariate_list <- list(   E0 = c("cnt_a", "cnt_b", "cnt_c", "bin_d", "bin_e"),   Emax = c("cnt_a", "cnt_b", "cnt_c", "bin_d", "bin_e") )  # add covariates to the base model using forward addition forward_model <- emax_scm_forward(   mod = base_model,   candidates = covariate_list,    threshold = .01 ) #> Warning: `nls()` did not converge forward_model #> Structural model: #>  #>   Exposure:       exp_1  #>   Response:       rsp_1  #>   Emax type:      hyperbolic  #>   Response type:  continuous #>  #> Covariate model: #>  #>   E0:       E0 ~ 1 + cnt_a  #>   Emax:     Emax ~ 1  #>   logEC50:  logEC50 ~ 1  #>  #> Model fit: #>  #>   Observations:         400  #>   Residual df:          396  #>   Residual std. error:  0.5108  #>   AIC:                  603.6431  #>  #> Coefficients (95% CI): #>  #>   label             estimate std_error lower  upper #> 1 E0_cnt_a             0.486    0.0116 0.463  0.509 #> 2 E0_Intercept         5.05     0.0759 4.91   5.20  #> 3 Emax_Intercept       9.97     0.112  9.75  10.2   #> 4 logEC50_Intercept    8.27     0.0394 8.19   8.35  #>  #> Use summary() for hypothesis tests.  # remove covariates from the forward model using backward deletion final_model <- emax_scm_backward(   mod = forward_model,   candidates = covariate_list,    threshold = .001 )  final_model #> Structural model: #>  #>   Exposure:       exp_1  #>   Response:       rsp_1  #>   Emax type:      hyperbolic  #>   Response type:  continuous #>  #> Covariate model: #>  #>   E0:       E0 ~ 1 + cnt_a  #>   Emax:     Emax ~ 1  #>   logEC50:  logEC50 ~ 1  #>  #> Model fit: #>  #>   Observations:         400  #>   Residual df:          396  #>   Residual std. error:  0.5108  #>   AIC:                  603.6431  #>  #> Coefficients (95% CI): #>  #>   label             estimate std_error lower  upper #> 1 E0_cnt_a             0.486    0.0116 0.463  0.509 #> 2 E0_Intercept         5.05     0.0759 4.91   5.20  #> 3 Emax_Intercept       9.97     0.112  9.75  10.2   #> 4 logEC50_Intercept    8.27     0.0394 8.19   8.35  #>  #> Use summary() for hypothesis tests.  # TRUE, parameters exponentiated renamed EC50 Hill respectively, confidence intervals transformed accordingly. Standard errors test statistics back-transformed scale available set NA. p_adjust Method adjusting p-values multiple comparisons, passed stats::p.adjust(). Defaults "none". Parameters suppressed p-values (see suppress_nonsensical) excluded adjustment set. affects p_value column independent simultaneous; see "Multiplicity: p-value adjustment versus simultaneous intervals" section . simultaneous TRUE, compute simultaneous (joint) confidence intervals using multivariate normal distribution via mvtnorm::qmvnorm(). gives wider intervals provide joint coverage conf_level across parameters simultaneously. Defaults FALSE. affects confidence-interval columns independent p_adjust; see "Multiplicity: p-value adjustment versus simultaneous intervals" section . suppress_nonsensical TRUE (default), suppress test statistic p-value logEC50_Intercept. logEC50 intercept estimated log-concentration scale, testing H0: logEC50 = 0 equivalent testing whether EC50 equals 1 concentration scale — threshold general pharmacometric meaning. confidence interval logEC50 always reported regardless setting. Pass suppress_nonsensical = FALSE restore raw test results. ... Ignored

text: simulate() confint(simultaneous = TRUE) / summary(simultaneous = TRUE) now degrade gracefully platforms mvtnorm shared object installed fails link runtime (observed clang-based Rhub builders). warning issued computation continues using base-R fallbacks: Cholesky-based multivariate normal sampling simulate(), Bonferroni-corrected normal quantile simultaneous confidence intervals. fallback intervals conservative valid (#52). tibble package now listed Suggests rather Imports, making genuine optional dependency. package functionality works without tibble installed (#24).

title: emaxnls 0.1.1.9000
text: Adds max_time argument emax_nls_options() emax_logistic_options() sets maximum elapsed time (seconds) model fitting. optimizer converged within limit terminated model treated non-converged, consistent convergence failure. Defaults Inf (limit). particularly useful running many models SCM procedure, single pathological fit can otherwise stall entire covariate search (#16). Adds emax_logistic() fitting binary-outcome Emax models using iterative reweighted least squares (IRLS), along emax_logistic_init() emax_logistic_options() initialization configuration. standard S3 methods (coef(), vcov(), confint(), residuals(), fitted(), predict(), anova(), logLik(), AIC(), BIC(), deviance(), simulate()) supported new emaxlogistic class. Redesigns print() summary() methods emaxnls emaxlogistic objects: print() now concise model overview showing structural covariate formulas, fit statistics (observations, residual df, sigma deviance, AIC), coefficient table estimates, standard errors, confidence intervals. Hypothesis tests shown print(); footer line directs users summary(). conf_level argument controls interval level (default 0.95). summary() gains three new arguments: suppress_nonsensical = TRUE: suppresses test statistic p-value logEC50_Intercept default. logEC50 intercept estimated log-concentration scale, testing H0: logEC50 = 0 corresponds testing EC50 = 1 concentration scale — threshold pharmacometric meaning. confidence interval logEC50 always reported. Pass suppress_nonsensical = FALSE restore raw test. p_adjust = "none": adjusts non-NA p-values via p.adjust() multiple-comparison correction; suppressed p-values excluded adjustment set. simultaneous = FALSE: TRUE, computes simultaneous Wald confidence intervals using mvtnorm::qmvnorm() correlation matrix vcov(), giving intervals joint coverage conf_level across parameters simultaneously. summary(back_transform = TRUE) now also sets test statistic NA back-transformed parameters, consistent standard error already NA back-transformed scale. confint() now falls back Wald intervals warning profile likelihood computation fails (can occur sigmoidal models).

text: Expands summary() documentation explain relationship p_adjust simultaneous arguments. two independent tools multiplicity — p_adjust corrects hypothesis-test p-values, simultaneous widens confidence intervals — use different machinery, reject/retain decisions need agree. new “Multiplicity: p-value adjustment versus simultaneous intervals” section spells argument changes, adjusted p-values simultaneous intervals may disagree, tool reach (#47).

title: emaxnls 0.1.1.9000
text: Fixes AIC() BIC() called multiple model arguments (#37). Fixes na.action parameter passed correctly emax_nls() emax_logistic() (#38). Fixes predict() omitting residual.scale return value se.fit = TRUE (#39). Fixes crashes emax_logistic_init() prevents Inf parameter bounds arising initialization (#40). Hardens .nls_call() avoid cryptic errors optimization fails, tightens argument validation emax_fun() (#41). Adds input validation binary response variable emax_logistic(), informative errors non-binary --range values (#42). Fixes validator error messages reference public API parameter names rather internal names (#43). Fixes NaN produced .binomial_deviance() predicted probabilities exactly 0 1 (boundary cases) (#44). confint() now accepts simultaneous argument, mirroring summary(). Previously confint(object, simultaneous = TRUE) silently ignored argument (swallowed ...) returned pointwise intervals. Setting simultaneous = TRUE now returns simultaneous (joint) Wald intervals match reported summary(object, simultaneous = TRUE) (#46).

title: emaxnls 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-30 Expanded description package. Fixes bug emax_nls_init() called manually. anova() now warns rather errors fewer two converged models supplied. Additional examples documentation. Improved unit tests.

title: emaxnls 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN submission. package provides tools fitting analyzing Emax dose-response models via nonlinear least squares.

title: emaxnls 0.1.0
text: emax_nls() fits continuous-response Emax models using NLS, supporting hyperbolic (E0 + Emax * x / (EC50 + x)) sigmoidal (E0 + Emax * x^Hill / (EC50^Hill + x^Hill)) model forms. emax_nls_options() configures optimization algorithm control parameters. Three algorithms supported via optim_method argument: "gauss" (Gauss-Newton, default), "port" (bounded nl2sol), "levenberg" (Levenberg-Marquardt via minpack.lm). emax_nls_init() generates starting values parameter bounds automatically data, including support categorical covariates. Users can also call directly inspect override initialization fitting.

title: emaxnls 0.1.0
text: Covariates can added structural parameter (E0, Emax, logEC50, logHill) via formula interface, e.g. emax_nls(rsp ~ dose, data = df, E0 = ~ group + age). emax_add_term() emax_remove_term() update fitted model adding removing single covariate term without refitting scratch.

title: emaxnls 0.1.0
text: emax_scm_forward() performs forward covariate selection, adding one term time likelihood-ratio test p-value stays threshold. emax_scm_backward() performs backward elimination, removing terms p-value exceeds threshold. emax_scm_history() returns sequence models tested SCM procedure, including AIC, BIC, convergence status candidate.

title: emaxnls 0.1.0
text: print() displays brief model summary. summary() produces coefficient table standard errors, confidence intervals, hypothesis tests. back_transform = TRUE argument re-expresses log-scaled parameters (logEC50, logHill) natural scales (EC50, Hill) output. coef() extracts parameter vector; supports back_transform = TRUE. vcov() returns estimated variance-covariance matrix. confint() computes profile-likelihood confidence intervals. residuals() fitted() return model residuals fitted values. predict() generates predictions, optionally standard errors. anova() compares nested models likelihood-ratio test. logLik(), AIC(), BIC(), sigma(), nobs(), df.residual() return standard model-fit statistics. simulate() draws Monte Carlo replicates resampling parameters asymptotic normal distribution estimates (requires mvtnorm). emax_fun() extracts standalone prediction function fitted model can evaluated arbitrary dose values parameter vectors. emax_converged() returns TRUE FALSE indicating whether optimizer converged. S3 methods handle non-convergent models gracefully returning emaxnls_null object rather erroring.

title: emaxnls 0.1.0
text: emax_df: synthetic dataset 400 observations across four dose groups (0, 100, 200, 300), continuous response, binary response, two exposure metrics, continuous binary covariates, categorical covariate.
